title: salix × arenicola
intro: Salix × arenicola, also known as the coastal willow or dwarf willow, is a hybrid plant species of the willow family (Salicaceae) that is native to the Pacific coast of North America. The medicinal properties of Salix × arenicola are attributed to its bark, which contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin that has anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving effects. The health benefits of the plant include reducing fever, relieving pain, and aiding in the treatment of rheumatism and other inflammatory conditions. Salix × arenicola can be prepared as a tincture, tea, or salve, and is often used in herbal medicine to promote relaxation and soothe skin irritations.
